29.reason: [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. When char is signed, EOF (which is -1) may be confused with a valid character byte 0xFF, causing premature loop termination or infinite loop.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. When char is signed, EOF (which is -1) may be confused with a valid character byte 0xFF, causing premature loop termination or infinite loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. This causes EOF comparison to fail on systems where char is unsigned, leading to infinite loop or incorrect behavior.]
